rows.notes: Clear year at top of page, large script '1838' with no amounts. Very high legibility and unambiguous.

rows.description: Statuta Dno. Victoriæ Schol. et Off.
rows.notes: Large cursive title below the year; letters partly flourished and some word endings ambiguous ('Schol. et Off.' is my reading). No monetary values on this line; interpreted as a section header.

rows.description: Stipend ad Post. Natio 1837 et Off
rows.notes: Description moderately legible. Numeric cluster at right reads clearly as '20 6 8' aligned in the three right-hand columns (pounds/shillings/pence). Column alignment verified by later total line. Shillings and pence values within valid ranges.
Amount '24 10 0' is clear and aligned; description is less certain — surname(s) read as 'Newington et Norton' but handwriting is slightly ambiguous around final letters.

Amounts '33 6 8' are clear and well aligned. Description reading 'Stipend Dno. Shires' fits the letter shapes; one or two letters in the surname are slightly unclear but context (a stipend line) supports this reading.
Amount '60 0 0' very clear. Description reads as an augmentation of a common (quondam = late) associated with a gentleman whose name I read as 'Capel' — several flourished letters are ambiguous.
Amount '30 9 2' aligns with the pounds/shillings/pence columns clearly. Description appears repeatedly on the page as 'Dno. Rectori ut supr.' and is legible enough to transcribe confidently.

Amount '89 3 8' is clear and consistent with column alignment. The description looks like a dividend/act line for the year 1837; several abbreviations are used and letters are somewhat ornate, hence reduced confidence.
Second line repeating the same amount '89 3 8' (clearly aligned). 'Ditto' and the following abbreviated locution 'P. Mich.' are legible though abbreviated and slightly flourished.
Amount '33 0 0' clear in pounds/shillings/pence columns. Description reads as a stipend entry for the year 1838; handwriting of year and 'Ann.' is clear.

Amount '42 0 3' is legible and columns aligned. The place/name reads like 'Bidlington' with trailing 'no 3 Ann. past' (handwriting for 'no' and 'past' is slightly unclear), so transcription of the description is tentative.
Repeated 'Dno. Rectori ut supr.' with the same amount '30 9 2' as earlier. Amount alignment and digits are clear; description matches earlier instances on the page.

rows.description: Stipend ad Post D. Bapt. et Off.
rows.notes: Amount '19 13 4' is clear and valid. Description reads as stipend for a post related to 'Bapt.' (Baptist) and 'Off.' (offices) — abbreviations and flourishes reduce certainty in exact wording.
Amount '33 6 8' clear. 'Ditto' indicates repeat of prior type; surname appears as 'Greves' or similar — the surname letters are flourishy and slightly uncertain.

rows.description: Augm. Common quondam Dno. Med. et Child.
rows.notes: Amount '36 0 0' is clearly written and aligned. Description includes 'Common quondam' and two names abbreviated 'Med. et Child.' — the personal names/abbreviations are partially unclear due to flourished script.
Amount '14 0 1' clear and validated. The place/name reads like 'Gillingston' and 'for 4th quarter Ann.' is legible though some letters in the place are uncertain.
Third occurrence of 'Dno. Rectori ut supr.' with amount '30 9 2'. Amount alignment consistent and digits clear; description matches prior identical lines.

rows.description: Stipend Quirincal? ad Est. S. Mich.
rows.notes: Amount '26 5 0' is clear. The descriptive phrase appears to refer to a stipend for an established (Est.) item linked to 'S. Mich.' (St. Michael) but the first word is fuzzy (read as 'Quirincal/Quinarian'); text is abbreviated and stylised.
Amount '35 14 9' is legible and in-range. Description likely names a common augmentation from 'Wittenham Longa' or 'Long' — last word spelling uncertain due to flourishy script.
Another recurring 'Dno. Rectori ut supr.' line with amount '30 9 2'. Amount and phrase consistent with previous instances; alignment confirms column placement.

Final boxed total clearly written as '698 7 5' (pounds/shillings/pence). The heavy underline/box provides an unambiguous alignment reference for the three right-hand columns; digits are crisp and values validate (shillings <20, pence <12).
